generalInquiriesText1: Before contacting our team, please click on either Individual or Couples Therapy to access key information that may quickly answer your questions.
generalInquiriesText2: If you are looking to inquire about appointments, you will be automatically directed to the team after selecting your preferred therapy type. This system is designed to answer quick questions first, helping us suggest the best match for your needs.
generalInquiriesText3: If your doubts are still not completely resolved after exploring those options, please click here. We will be more than happy to contact you as soon as possible to provide any further guidance you may need.
medicareText: To access Medicare rebates for your sessions, you will need a referral and a specific structured care plan from your General Practitioner (GP). Depending on your diagnosis, your GP may issue a Mental Health Care Plan (MHCP), which covers up to 10 rebated individual sessions per calendar year, or an Eating Disorder Management Plan (EDMP), which provides access to up to 40 rebated psychological treatment sessions within a 12-month period. Our practice ethos is to keep quality mental health care accessible; therefore, we always aim to ensure your out-of-pocket (gap) expense does not exceed $100 AUD per session. If you do not hold a Medicare card or lack coverage through alternative third-party funding—such as an Employee Assistance Program (EAP) or private health insurance—we will match you with a qualified professional whose private fee structure aligns perfectly with your budget, ensuring you still only pay $100 AUD out of pocket.
couplesTherapyFeesText: Please note that Couples Therapy is billed at a flat rate of $140 AUD per session. Unlike individual therapy, couples and family consultations are strictly private services in Australia. This means they are completely out-of-pocket expenses and cannot be subsidised by Medicare rebates, EAP funding, or other institutional third-party payees.
ndisFundingText: For participants under the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS), we offer services aligned with your specific funding goals. Depending on your NDIS plan, you can request treatment funded under Psychosocial Recovery Coaching (focused on capacity-building and resilience for severe mental health conditions) or standard Capacity-Building Support (aimed at developing independence and daily living skills). Because NDIS pricing limits and regulations differ significantly based on the practitioner's credentials, we highly recommend checking your NDIS plan management type (Self-Managed, Plan-Managed, or NDIA-Managed). You must verify whether your specific plan allows you to engage a counsellor for these supports or if it strictly mandates the services of a registered psychologist, as hourly rates and internal clinic assignment protocols will vary accordingly.
couplesTherapyCost: Sessions are billed at $140 AUD and can be in-person or online.
couplesTherapyTitle: Couples Therapy with Matías De Ambrosio: A Relational and Communication-Focused Approach
couplesTherapyIntro: Matías De Ambrosio offers a distinctive and highly specialized approach to couples therapy, moving away from conventional methods. Rather than assigning standard homework tasks or behavioral exercises to perform at home, his practice shifts the focus entirely toward the live dynamic within the therapeutic session.
couplesCoreFocusText: The clinical interviews are structured around the immediate emotional and communicative reality of the couple. Matías focuses deeply on:
couplesCoreFocus1: Affective Exploration: Investigating what each member of the couple genuinely feels and expresses in the room.
couplesCoreFocus2: Communication Patterns: Deconstructing how the couple interacts and rebuilding a shared language.
couplesCoreFocus3: Conflict Resolution: Navigating disagreements, addressing underlying tensions, and fostering mutual understanding.
couplesSpecializationText: Matías's practice is highly inclusive and adapted to contemporary relationship structures, providing safe, expert containment for a diverse range of clients:
couplesSpecialization1: Diverse Couple Configurations: Extensive experience working with bilingual couples (navigating cultural and linguistic nuances), traditional couples, LGBTQ+ relationships, and neurodivergent couples (tailoring communication styles to varied cognitive processing profiles).
couplesSpecialization2: Complex Family Dynamics: Deep clinical understanding of intricate systemic family structures and interpersonal conflicts.
couplesConclusion: Through this clinical framework, therapy becomes a profound space for insight and structural change, focusing on real-time resolution rather than prescriptive behavioral management.
matiasBio1: Matías is a highly qualified Mental Health Professional with over 15 years of clinical experience. While he holds a registered and specialized license as a Clinical Psychologist in Argentina, he operates within our clinic in Australia as a Senior Counsellor. Drawing on systemic, relational, and communication-focused frameworks, he specializes in deconstructing complex interpersonal dynamics and facilitating conflict resolution. His extensive clinical background includes managing acute psychiatric presentations, high-conflict relationship breakdowns, domestic violence, and family complexity.
matiasHospitalBio: He worked at the General Acute Care Hospital of Berisso, Argentina, for four years, followed by another four years of clinical practice at the Private Neuropsychiatric Clinic La Plata, Argentina. This extensive institutional experience within both public health and acute psychiatric frameworks strengthened his capacity for crisis intervention, comprehensive risk assessment, and complex case management.
matiasAcademicBio: Alongside his clinical practice, Matías has a prominent background in academia. He has served as a university lecturer and Chief of Practical Works in psychology and complex systems curricula, as well as a scientific researcher in community health. Furthermore, he brings strong experience in professional mentorship, having supervised and served as a jury member for numerous bachelor theses for psychology students overseas. He is also a published author, with chapters and papers focusing on psychosis, clinical transference, and mental health frameworks.
matiasCulturalBio: Originally from Argentina, where he graduated from the National University of La Plata and earned his specialization as a Clinical Psychology Specialist, Matías offers sessions in Spanish and English. He provides culturally responsive and linguistically accessible care, maintaining a reflective, ethical, and collaborative therapeutic environment tailored to the unique recovery trajectory of each individual, couple, or family.
successMessage: Thank you for sending your request to be contacted by the Counselling and Clinical Psychology WA team. You will hear from us soon to answer all your queries, discuss possible appointment times, and explore options for you to start therapy as soon as possible.
indivTherapySubtitle: Let us help you find the professional that best fits your needs.
indivTherapyIntro: Click the button below to answer four quick questions, allowing us to tailor our recommendation just for you. Rest reassured that after our suggestion you will be able to accept it or to ask for another professional of your choice.
q1Title: What is your primary reason for seeking consultation? (Required)
q1Subtitle: If you have a relevant prior diagnosis you would like to share, please enter it here. (Optional)
q3Title: What is your preferred language for the sessions?
q4Title: Do you require formal reports for an institution, or will sessions be funded through a third party, such as WorkCover, DVA, or an Employee Assistance Program (EAP)?
optQ4B: Yes, an institution/workplace will be paying for the sessions.
celesteBio1: Celeste is a PhD-qualified Clinical Psychologist, with 17 years of clinical experience. She supports adults and teens aged 16 years and over, drawing on trauma-informed, attachment-based, and strengths-focused frameworks. She has experience working with most presentations, including Complex PTSD, BPD, Bipolar Disorder, ADHD and Autism. Celeste also works with Dissociative Disorders and depersonalization. Moreover, she offers gender and neuro affirming therapy. If you have NDIS, been hospitalized or in psychiatric treatment, don't hesitate booking for a session.
celesteBio2: Celeste supervises papers for several peer-reviewed Journals of Psychology from different countries, as well as bachelor and PhD thesis from Argentinian colleagues. You can read her papers on dreams, death, psychosis and more issues on scholar google.
celesteBio3: Originally from Argentina, she offers sessions in Spanish and English, providing culturally responsive and linguistically accessible care within a bilingual therapeutic space. She is committed to providing a reflective, ethical, collaborative, and culturally safe therapeutic environment, grounded in respect and inclusive practice.
sessionPreference: Preferred format for your first session:
consentInstruction: Please check Yes or No for the following optional statements:
clinicalAiConsent: Clinical AI Documentation Consent: We consent to the use of secure, confidential Clinical AI tools to assist in generating our clinical records for enhanced accuracy and quality.
termsCouplesText1: 1. Privacy & Data Management: We understand that the collection and secure storage of our personal information is required to provide psychological services, and all data is managed in accordance with the Privacy Act 1988.
termsCouplesText2: 2. Limitations of Confidentiality: We acknowledge that our information is strictly confidential, except where there is a legal obligation to disclose (e.g., immediate risk of serious harm to oneself, the other partner, or others, or a court subpoena).
termsCouplesText3: 3. Couples Therapy Framework: We understand that in couples therapy, the relationship is the primary focus of the intervention. Information shared within joint sessions is managed within the context of the couple's shared therapeutic space.
consentSupervision: Consent to Case Supervision: I consent to my case being discussed under clinical supervision strictly under conditions of complete anonymity.
communicationConsent: Communication Consent: I consent to be contacted by the clinic via phone calls and text messages (SMS) regarding appointments and administration.
ndisConsent: NDIS Eligibility Referral Consent: I consent to be contacted by our partner organisation, Innovative Mentoring, in the event that I am found suitable to apply for the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS).
termsIndivText1: 1. Clinical Records & Privacy: I understand that the collection and secure storage of my personal and health information is a legal requirement for providing psychological services, and all data is managed in accordance with the Privacy Act 1988.
termsIndivText2: 2. Limitations of Confidentiality: I acknowledge that my information is strictly confidential, except where there is a legal obligation to disclose (e.g., immediate risk of serious harm to myself or others, or a court subpoena).
